name: environs (Environment Variable Parsing with Type Coercion)

agent_readiness.notes: Typed environment variable parsing with validation. from environs import Env. env = Env(). env.read_env('.env'). PORT = env.int('PORT', 5000). DEBUG = env.bool('DEBUG', False). DATABASE_URL = env.str('DATABASE_URL'). LOG_LEVEL = env.log_level('LOG_LEVEL', logging.INFO). Types: str, int, float, bool, list, dict, path, url, email, uuid, log_level, timedelta. Prefix: env = Env(prefix='APP_'). Validation: env.str('KEY', validate=validate.Length(min=3)). marshmallow validators. Namespace groups. Reads .env files.

gotchas: Niche overlap with pydantic-settings — choose one
env.read_env() must be called before accessing vars
Validation via marshmallow validators is less discoverable
pydantic-settings provides better IDE support and type checking
best_when: Simple 12-factor app configuration from environment variables
When typed .env parsing is needed without pydantic complexity
Flask/Django apps needing typed environment config
Quick scripts with typed environment variable access
avoid_when: Complex nested config (use pydantic-settings or dynaconf)
When full type checking and IDE support matters (pydantic-settings better)
summary: environs provides typed environment variable parsing with .env file support. env.int('PORT', 5000), env.bool('DEBUG'), env.url('API_URL'). Types: str, int, float, bool, list, path, url, email, uuid, log_level, timedelta. Validation via marshmallow. Prefix grouping. MIT. Simpler than pydantic-settings for 12-factor apps. For FastAPI projects with type checking, prefer pydantic-settings.
